channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: U.S. added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KTLA 5

item.title: UK's Starmer convenes 'coalition of the willing' for second meeting to discuss Ukraine
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is convening a second meeting of global leaders Saturday to discuss the developments regarding the war in Ukraine, following another frantic week of diplomacy aimed at getting Russia to back a 30-day ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned next week after a federal appeals court on Friday vacated a preliminary injunction granted by a lower judge. With a March 18 date hastily nearing, attorneys for Jessie Hoffman Jr.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: The trend uses her song "Anxiety" and spoofs a scene from "The Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" where Smith's character sneaks up on Ali's, who's dancing with her headphones on, and starts to mimic her.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, KTLA 5

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, Fox 5

item.title: Aircraft catches fire after landing in Denver, sending passengers onto wing as smoke engulfs plane
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A fire on an American Airlines plane after it diverted mid-flight and landed at Denver International Airport sent passengers fleeing onto a wing in a fraught evacuation amid billowing clouds of smoke. Airport officials said 12 people were taken to hospitals with minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, SARAH BRUMFIELD, JESSE BEDAYN, COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: 911 audio released in Idaho student murders
item.description: Authorities have unveiled the recording of a panicked 911 call placed after the stabbings of four University of Idaho students in November 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate unanimously approves fix to prevent $1B cuts for District of Columbia, sending bill to House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate swiftly approved legislation late Friday that would allow the District of Columbia to keep its budget intact, rather than roll back to 2024, fixing a provision that had drawn protests from the mayor and residents warning it would require $1 billion in cuts to services.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge considers blocking DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge is considering whether to temporarily block Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Social Security Administration systems that hold sensitive data on millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cause of death for Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa's dog released
item.description: An examination of the dog found dead along with actor Gene Hackman and his wife in their Santa Fe home shows dehydration and starvation were likely what led to the animal’s demise.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DJ Daniel sworn in as honorary police officer more than 800 times
item.description: He has a rare brain and spine cancer with no cure. His father, Theodis Daniel, says DJ fights for his life daily.
item.dc:creator: Myriam Samake, NewsNation

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Air taxis flying over Austin could transform future commutes
item.description: "You can come and train in VR and in less than an hour you can solo fly vertical lift aircraft," Chasen said.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KTLA 5

item.title: Founding girl group member reveals she's been living in her car
item.description: "If you would have said to me while I was En Vogue, 'You're going to be living in your car one day,' I'd be like, 'Huh?"
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Columbia University says it expelled some students who seized building last year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University has expelled or suspended some students who took over a campus building during pro-Palestinian protests last spring and temporarily revoked the diplomas of others who have since graduated, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Columbia announces disciplinary action for students who took over campus building
item.description: The discipline includes “multi-year suspensions, temporary degree revocations, and expulsions,” according to the school.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Jewish protesters flood Trump Tower's lobby to demand Mahmoud Khalil's release
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Demonstrators from a Jewish group filled the lobby of Trump Tower on Thursday to denounce the immigration arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a pro-Palestinian activist who helped lead protests against Israel at Columbia University.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capri Sun launches glow-in-the-dark Moon Punch for lunar eclipse
item.description: Capri Sun has launched its first-ever glow-in-the-dark pouch, known as Moon Pouch, in honor of the upcoming Total Lunar Eclipse. The total lunar eclipse, also known as a Blood Moon, occurs when the Earth is fully positioned between the sun and the moon.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge orders Trump to rehire probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco ordered President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies, blasting their tactics Thursday as he slowed the new president's dramatic down
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Federal student loan site down Wednesday, a day after layoffs gutted Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An hours-long outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Connecticut woman arrested after stepson allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: A Connecticut woman accused of kidnapping a man and holding him captive for over 20 years was arrested Wednesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump must reinstate many fired federal employees
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that the Trump administration must reinstate probationary government employees fired unlawfully at several agencies, lambasting the Justice Department at a hearing for a “sham” gambit that enabled a key official to avoid testifying in the case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit accuses man of killing, eating patients at SC mental health facility
item.description: The case against a South Carolina mental health facility where a man is accused of murdering and cannibalizing patients could be heading to trial.
item.dc:creator: Chase Laudenslager, ABC 4

item.title: Protesters supporting Mahmoud Khalil occupy Trump Tower lobby
item.description: Pro-Mahmoud Khalil protesters occupied the lobby area of Trump Tower on Manhattan's Fifth Avenue on Thursday, chanting for his release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic-led states sue to block Trump administration layoffs at the Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of Democratic-led states is challenging the Trump administration’s sweeping layoffs across the Education Department, saying it amounts to an illegal dismantling of an agency created by Congress.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: A breakdown of major EPA deregulatory moves around water, air, climate
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeldin on Wednesday announced nearly three dozen deregulatory moves that he said would spur the U.S. economy by rolling back rules that have unfairly burdened industry.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, ALEXA ST. JOHN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SpaceX Dragon launch to relieve stranded astronauts is delayed again
item.description: Less than an hour before liftoff, the SpaceX Dragon mission to provide assistance to two astronauts was aborted yet again, this time for a ground equipment issue.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: T-Mobile customers to get payments up to $25K next month after data breach: Here's who qualifies
item.description: The $350 million T-Mobile agreed to pay in a 2022 class action settlement will finally start going out to customers next month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9
